app.title: PathLab
app.heroText: Draw walls and weighted terrain, move start and target, and compare how different algorithms search.

onboarding.title: Ready to explore?
onboarding.text: Draw walls or weighted terrain first. Then move start and target if needed and run an algorithm.
steps: Move start and target if needed
Run one algorithm or compare all algorithms

status.initial: Draw walls, choose an algorithm, then run the visualization.
status.reset: Grid reset. Ready for a new visualization.
status.compared: All algorithms were compared on the same grid.

tip.title: Beginner tip
tip.text: Place water or mud in the direct path. Then it becomes easier to see why Dijkstra and A* care about costs.

bfs.name: BFS
bfs.description: BFS searches layer by layer and finds the shortest path by number of steps on a normal grid. It ignores weighted terrain, so it can miss the cheapest path.

dfs.name: DFS
dfs.description: DFS goes as far as possible in one direction first. It clearly shows depth-first behavior, but it does not guarantee the shortest or cheapest path.

dijkstra.name: Dijkstra
dijkstra.description: Dijkstra always expands the cheapest known cell next. That lets it avoid water and mud when a longer route is cheaper overall.

astar.name: A*
astar.description: A* uses real costs like Dijkstra, but also estimates the direction to the target. This often makes it more focused and visit fewer cells.
